description_long: This is a two-story brick house with a hip roof covered in light brown shingles. The facade features a covered porch supported by slender columns, and a main entryway that is recessed beneath the porch roof. Windows are arranged in a mostly symmetrical pattern, with double-hung white-framed windows on each visible side. The exterior walls are clad in a reddish-brown brick pattern, giving the house a traditional and sturdy appearance. The roof is broad and slopes gently on all sides, typical of suburban residential architecture. The house appears to be designed for a single-family and would fit well in a quiet urban neighborhood.
description: A two-story brick house with a hip roof, covered porch, and white-framed windows arranged symmetrically. The exterior is traditional and sturdy, suitable for a suburban neighborhood.
description_view_0: Front view showing the covered porch, main entryway, and two sets of white-framed windows above the porch.
description_view_1: Side view with three white-framed windows and a clear view of the hip roof's slope.
description_view_2: Rear view with a single white-framed window and uninterrupted brick wall.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with three white-framed windows and partial view of the porch roof.
attribute_entryway_design: Recessed main door under a covered porch with slender columns and no visible steps or decorative elements.
